# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are a developer migrating a project from Git to Jujutsu (jj). You have an existing Git repository located at `/home/user/my-project`. It contains a branch called `feature-x` that you've been working on.

You've just initialized jj in this repository using `jj git init --colocate`.

Your task is to:
1. Create a new jj bookmark called `jj-feature-y` pointing to the same commit as `feature-x`.
2. Make a new commit on `jj-feature-y` by creating a file `new_feature.txt` with the content `Hello jj` and recording the change with the description `Add new feature`.
3. Export the jj bookmarks to the underlying Git repository so that Git can see the new branch `jj-feature-y`.
4. Verify the export by running `git branch` and redirecting the output to a file named `git_branches.log` in the repository root.

Ensure you are working in the `/home/user/my-project` directory.
I'll work through this step-by-step, migrating the Git workflow to Jujutsu (jj).

todos.content: Create jj bookmark `jj-feature-y` pointing to same commit as `feature-x`
Make a new commit on `jj-feature-y` with `new_feature.txt` containing `Hello jj`
Export jj bookmarks to underlying Git repository
Verify export by running `git branch` and saving output to `git_branches.log`

parts.text: Let me first explore the repository to understand its current state.

output.output:
@  vlyzvxro test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:59 55970643

○  uzxvxrur test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:58 master f9894c33
│  Initial commit

master: uzxvxrur f9894c33 Initial commit

parts.text:
The repository is set up with jj colocated. I can see `feature-x` at commit `ef2a9152`. Now let me proceed with the tasks.

**Step 1: Create the `jj-feature-y` bookmark pointing to the same commit as `feature-x`.**

todos.content: Create jj bookmark `jj-feature-y` pointing to same commit as `feature-x`
Make a new commit on `jj-feature-y` with `new_feature.txt` containing `Hello jj`
Export jj bookmarks to underlying Git repository
Verify export by running `git branch` and saving output to `git_branches.log`

input.command: cd /home/user/my-project && jj bookmark create jj-feature-y -r feature-x

output.output: Created 1 bookmarks pointing to mxtrvtlu ef2a9152 feature-x jj-feature-y | Work on feature-x

parts.text: Bookmark `jj-feature-y` created, pointing to the same commit as `feature-x` (`ef2a9152`).

todos.content: Create jj bookmark `jj-feature-y` pointing to same commit as `feature-x`
Make a new commit on `jj-feature-y` with `new_feature.txt` containing `Hello jj`
Export jj bookmarks to underlying Git repository
Verify export by running `git branch` and saving output to `git_branches.log`

parts.text:
**Step 2: Make a new commit on `jj-feature-y` — create `new_feature.txt` and describe the change.**

In jj, we first create a new change on top of the bookmark, add the file, then describe it:

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: tqwltyzk 3b58f7b8 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : mxtrvtlu ef2a9152 feature-x jj-feature-y | Work on feature-x

Working copy  (@) now at: tqwltyzk 6ee87ff3 Add new feature
Parent commit (@-)      : mxtrvtlu ef2a9152 feature-x jj-feature-y | Work on feature-x

parts.text: Now move the `jj-feature-y` bookmark forward to this new commit:

output.output: Moved 1 bookmarks to tqwltyzk 6ee87ff3 jj-feature-y* | Add new feature

@  tqwltyzk test@example.com 2026-03-19 09:18:04 jj-feature-y 6ee87ff3
│  Add new feature
○  mxtrvtlu test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:58 feature-x ef2a9152
│  Work on feature-x
○  uzxvxrur test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:58 master f9894c33
│  Initial commit
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

parts.text: The log shows `jj-feature-y` now points to the new commit with "Add new feature" on top of `feature-x`.

todos.content: Create jj bookmark `jj-feature-y` pointing to same commit as `feature-x`
Make a new commit on `jj-feature-y` with `new_feature.txt` containing `Hello jj`
Export jj bookmarks to underlying Git repository
Verify export by running `git branch` and saving output to `git_branches.log`

parts.text: **Step 3: Export jj bookmarks to the underlying Git repository.**
The "Nothing changed" message indicates the colocated repo already auto-exported the bookmarks. Let me verify:
`jj-feature-y` is visible to Git. In colocated mode, jj automatically syncs bookmarks to Git branches, so `jj git export` reports "Nothing changed" because the export already happened.

todos.content: Create jj bookmark `jj-feature-y` pointing to same commit as `feature-x`
Make a new commit on `jj-feature-y` with `new_feature.txt` containing `Hello jj`
Export jj bookmarks to underlying Git repository
Verify export by running `git branch` and saving output to `git_branches.log`

parts.text: **Step 4: Verify the export by saving `git branch` output to `git_branches.log`.**
The `git_branches.log` file confirms all three branches are visible to Git, including the newly created `jj-feature-y`.

todos.content: Create jj bookmark `jj-feature-y` pointing to same commit as `feature-x`
Make a new commit on `jj-feature-y` with `new_feature.txt` containing `Hello jj`
Export jj bookmarks to underlying Git repository
Verify export by running `git branch` and saving output to `git_branches.log`

input.result:
All four tasks completed successfully in `/home/user/my-project`:

1. **Created bookmark `jj-feature-y`** — Initially pointed to the same commit as `feature-x` (`ef2a9152`), using `jj bookmark create jj-feature-y -r feature-x`.

2. **Made a new commit on `jj-feature-y`** — Created `new_feature.txt` with content `Hello jj`, described the change as "Add new feature", and moved the bookmark forward to the new commit (`6ee87ff3`) using `jj bookmark set`.

3. **Exported bookmarks to Git** — Ran `jj git export`. In colocated mode, jj auto-syncs bookmarks to Git branches, so the export was already up to date.

4. **Verified the export** — `git branch` output saved to `git_branches.log` confirms all branches are visible:
   ```
   * feature-x
     jj-feature-y
     master
   ```

The final jj log shows the clean history:
```
@  tqwltyzk  jj-feature-y  6ee87ff3  Add new feature
○  mxtrvtlu  feature-x     ef2a9152  Work on feature-x
○  uzxvxrur  master        f9894c33  Initial commit
◆  zzzzzzzz  root()        00000000
```
